recommendations.note: The wiki calls this "probably one of the best ways to farm Ferrite" (4,000+ per 10-minute rotation); Ferrite pours from the enemies you kill, so a Nekros loot squad and a Resource Drop Booster push the numbers even higher.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Earth — Tikal (Dark Sector Excavation, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: A long Steel Path Excavation keeps enemies spawning against the excavators; Ferrite is a common enemy drop, so Steel Path density, a Resource Drop Booster and a loot frame all stack for a sustained squad farm.
Low-level Corpus Defense on Venus, one of Alloy Plate’s signature planets, gives steady drops from the very start of the star chart.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Zariman Ten Zero — Tuvul Commons (Void Cascade)
recommendations.note: Endless Void Cascade enemy density makes this the highest-yield Alloy Plate farm for geared squads; Ceres Gabii Steel Path with a booster is a solid alternative.

A classic low-level Infested farm: Akkad’s dense Infested waves pour Nano Spores as enemy drops, so a Nekros/Khora loot squad and a Resource Drop Booster stack up huge hauls in just a few Defense rounds.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Saturn — Piscinas (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: Steel Path Infested density with a Khora/Nekros loot squad on this Dark Sector node yields large Nano Spore hauls per rotation.
A safe low-level Dark Sector Survival on one of Salvage’s signature planets; Salvage drops from the enemies you kill, so a Nekros loot squad and a Resource Drop Booster increase the haul.

A high-throughput Corpus Mobile Defense; Salvage is a common enemy drop, so pairing Steel Path enemy density with a Nekros/Khora loot squad and a Resource Drop Booster is the fastest way to bank thousands per run.
Short, low-level Corpus Defense on one of Circuits’ two signature planets; Circuits drop from the enemies you kill, so a Resource Drop Booster and a loot frame add to every wave.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Ceres — Gabii (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: The wiki flags Ceres Dark Sector nodes as the higher-drop-rate Circuits spot; on Steel Path the Grineer density plus a Resource Drop Booster and Nekros make Gabii a strong endless squad farm.
Uranus is Polymer Bundle’s primary planet, so nearly every resource drop here is a bundle; Ophelia also nets Tellurium on the side.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Uranus — Assur (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: Same Dark Sector Grineer Galleon chokepoints used for Plastids double as an efficient Steel Path Polymer Bundle squad farm.
Accessible low-level Corpus Defense; Phobos is one of the few main planets with a decent Plastids drop chance early on.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Uranus — Assur (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: Plastids are notoriously low-yield, so a Dark Sector Steel Path Survival squad farm (with Nekros/Hydroid) is worth the endgame investment.
The wiki cites roughly 250 Rubedo per 5 minutes here on a signature Rubedo planet; Rubedo drops from the enemies you kill, so a Nekros loot squad and a Resource Drop Booster raise the yield further.

The wiki recommends Orokin Void endless missions for 1,000+ Rubedo per 20 minutes; Ani is a standard Void Survival for that squad farm, and since Rubedo is an enemy drop the Steel Path passive and a Resource Drop Booster compound it.
The wiki notes Morphics drop far more often on Mars than elsewhere; this quick Extermination lets a Resource Drop Booster and Nekros Desecrate multiply the Morphics that Mars enemies drop.

Mars stays the best Morphics planet at every stage; Steel Path Disruption keeps dense Grineer (and Demolysts) spawning, so a Resource Drop Booster and a loot frame compound the enemy-dropped Morphics across rotations.

Quick low-level Grineer Extermination on one of Gallium’s two signature planets; Mars enemies drop Gallium from the region resource pool, so a Resource Drop Booster and Nekros Desecrate increase the haul.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Uranus — Titania (Assassination, Tyl Regor, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: Tyl Regor and the Grineer around him drop Gallium as enemy drops on Uranus, Gallium’s other signature planet; Steel Path density plus a Resource Drop Booster makes repeat boss runs efficient for a squad.
Breaking the green-glowing resource containers in the Grineer cave tileset on this quick Capture run guarantees Neurodes, no boss or high level required.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Lua — Circulus (Void Storm/Fissure Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: One of the most popular full-squad Steel Path fissure farms; dense Sentient/Corpus enemy spawns drop Neurodes alongside Rubedo and relics.
Low-level Corpus Survival that new players can rotate for a while; enemy drops feed Neural Sensors at an accessible level range.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Jupiter — Cameria (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: Dark Sector +20% resource drop chance plus Steel Path enemy density against Infested hordes makes this the best endgame squad spot.

The Void’s Corrupted enemies carry a higher Control Module drop chance than any main-planet foe; Hepit is a fast, low-level Void Capture to farm them, and since it is an enemy drop a Resource Drop Booster and Nekros Desecrate both help.

An endless Void Survival keeps a Steel Path squad killing Corrupted for the longest uninterrupted Control Module stream; the drops are enemy-based, so a Resource Drop Booster, the Steel Path passive and a loot frame all stack.
Fast, repeatable boss kill with a solid Orokin Cell drop chance; accessible as soon as Saturn is unlocked.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Ceres — Gabii (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: Dark Sector +35% resource drop chance stacks with the Steel Path drop-chance passive and Resourceful Retriever/boosters for sustained squad farming.
Reliable early Oxium Osprey spawns; the wiki cites roughly 80 Oxium over 10 waves on this low-level Corpus Defense.
Higher-level Corpus spawns more Oxium Ospreys per minute; running long Survival rotations beats resetting missions repeatedly.

The wiki also documents Tellurium coming from enemies in any "Submersible"-tagged tileset (Uranus’ Grineer Sealab is one), so this Survival’s ever-growing enemy density genuinely adds to the haul on top of its resource caches, unlike a pure container farm.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Uranus — Assur (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: The wiki states Steel Path’s higher enemy density and resource drop chance give "anecdotally around 4x" the Tellurium of a regular run on this Sealab tileset, so — unlike most Uranus resources — boosters and the Steel Path passive are worth stacking here.

Hexenon has no resource-deposit variant at all — the wiki notes it comes only from Amalgam enemies (~7.2% drop chance) and mission-completion rewards, so this accessible low-level Corpus Defense is an early, enemy-drop-based source.

The wiki calls out Ganymede’s Disruption Amalgam spawns as the most consistent Hexenon source (27–30% per rotation); since it’s purely an enemy drop, Resource Drop Chance Boosters and the Steel Path passive both meaningfully increase yield here.

Mutagen Sample is a Deimos regional drop — the wiki classes it “uncommon” on Deimos (vs “rare” elsewhere), making Deimos the best sustained source. Farm any endless Infested node — Terrorem (Survival) or Hyf (Defense) — or Cambion Drift bounties/free roam, killing in bulk. These are in-mission enemy/tileset pickups, so a Resource Drop Booster and the Steel Path resource passive DO boost them. No Deimos yet? Eris — Naeglar (Hive Sabotage) drops a Rotation-B cache (~15.5% for 3) as a quest-free fallback.

Steel Path Deimos scales the same regional farm: denser, tankier Infested drop more samples per minute, and the Steel Path Resource Drop Chance Booster stacks on the in-mission pickups. For guaranteed bulk instead, run the Eris Sabotage caches (Naeglar/Candiru/Lepis/Psoro/Viver, Rotation B ~15.5% for 3) or Deimos — Formido (Rotation A ~14.3% for 3) — but caches are end-of-mission rewards, so only the Steel Path drop-chance booster affects those, not resource boosters.

Cervantes’ Rotation B resource cache is a reliably documented Detonite Ampule source (~12.2%) and a fast Sabotage to repeat early. Resource caches are reward tables, so a booster and the Steel Path passive don’t affect this particular pickup — for a boosted enemy farm, see the endgame spot.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Saturn — Piscinas (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: A Dark Sector Steel Path Survival where Grineer density is high; Detonite Ampule drops from those enemies (it’s in Saturn’s region resource pool), so a Resource Drop Booster, the Steel Path passive and a Nekros loot squad all raise the yield.

A low-level Dark Sector Defense on one of Fieldron Sample’s signature planets; it drops from the enemies you kill (it’s in Mars’ region resource pool), so a Resource Drop Booster and a loot frame add to each round.

recommendations.nodeLabel: Neptune — Kelashin (Dark Sector Survival, Steel Path)
recommendations.note: A Dark Sector Steel Path Survival on another Fieldron Sample planet; the Infested density there drops Fieldron Sample as enemy loot, so a Resource Drop Booster, the Steel Path passive and a Nekros loot squad all stack for a squad farm.
Every non-Defense Void tileset has ~2 static Argon Deposit containers worth 1-2 crystals each, no high level needed; remember they decay daily at 00:00 GMT.
Endless Void Survival lets a squad bank as many crystals as they can spend before the daily decay; Resourceful Retriever and boosters help enemy drops here.
